# Loom LLM Context

Loom is contracts-first wallet infrastructure for Ethereum. It aims to let
wallet builders deliver mainstream UX while preserving user sovereignty,
privacy, recovery, verification, and permissionless exit.

## Current implemented surfaces

- Immutable LoomAccount with no developer admin, proxy upgrade path, or
  privileged factory operation.
- ERC-4337 v0.9 validation, ERC-1271, direct signed execution, and atomic
  single/batch execution.
- P-256/WebAuthn validator, multi-passkey threshold validator, ECDSA validator,
  bounded session validator, and granular session validator.
- PolicyHook for low-risk call classification, token limits, and paymaster
  restrictions.
- RecoveryManager with guardian threshold, visible delay, expiry, cancellation,
  complete validator-set replacement, and fresh guardian-root rotation.
- VaultHook for daily spending limits and delayed long-term asset withdrawals.
- Sovereign migration with destination code/config binding, delay, expiry,
  cancellation, hook enforcement, and atomic execution.
- L1 keystore surface and proof-gated sync module; production proof verifiers
  remain pending.
- @loom/privacy host runtime skeleton for Kohaku-backed clients. The runtime
  requires explicit provider consent and metadata-budget checks before network
  access.

## Non-goals and warnings

- Loom is pre-audit and not production-ready.
- Do not describe Loom as a finished wallet app.
- Do not claim live cross-wallet gas superiority. Use the repository gas
  snapshot for Loom-only measurements and mark cross-wallet benchmarks as
  pending until the shared harness exists.
- Do not make Kohaku, Railgun, Aztec, privacy pools, RPC providers, relayers,
  indexers, paymasters, or recovery coordinators mandatory account authority.
- Do not add a global identity registry that publicly links all user accounts.

## Website source

The Vocs website lives in ./docs/site and is deployed by
.github/workflows/pages.yml.
